## [12:02 - 13:15]

it comes down to the scientific side of testing. So take your ideas, try to avoid just putting things in the page because you've seen it elsewhere and do a split-test. Don't just assume that because the biggest store in your niche is using this new function, this new feature that it's gonna work for you, test it. Your users are probably slightly different, your marketing's different, your product page and things are different. You can't literally just copy your competitor and just hit it out of the park like that. What you can do is funnel hack your competitor, go through their entire funnel, buy the product, see what you like and don't like about it, and then apply that transformation to your own funnel and do it in a testing mode as well. So if you have an existing page, the simplest way to do a split-test is to take your existing page, create a variant of that page and test it and see what the performance looks like when you run your new treatment against your existing page and use that to incrementally improve your performance because that will keep your ad costs low, that will improve your CPM because the engagement improves on your site as well and you'll benefit from that in your scaling as well.
